{{Quote|Return mesa to my city, and mesa will swear allegiance to yousa.|Hoxie, to Boss Gallo|<ref name="SW:GB" />}}
'''Hoxie''' was a [[Ankura/Legends|Ankura]] [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] who was the [[Boss/Legends|boss]] of the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Otoh Raban]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3000 BBY]]. During a period of [[War of the Gungan tribes|war]] at that [[Time/Legends|time]] between the Gungan tribes, Hoxie was [[Kidnapping of Hoxie|kidnapped]] by the [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] Boss [[Rogoe/Legends|Rogoe]] to keep Otoh Raban in the war, leaving the city leaderless. Eventually, [[Gallo/Legends|Gallo]], a Gungan boss who was uniting the tribes against Rogoe, heard about Hoxie's kidnapping from a [[Unidentified Gungan villager (Otoh Raban)|villager]] living in Otoh Raban. In order to gain the city's allegiance, Gallo rescued Hoxie from the [[Rogoe's stronghold|prison camp]], and Otoh Raban contributed more troops to Gallo's [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|Grand Army]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Very recently, our Boss was taken prisoner by one of the bombad evil tribes.|A villager living in Otoh Raban, speaking of Hoxie|<ref name="SW:GB" />}}
[[File:Otoh_Raban.png|thumb|left|250px|Hoxie's city, Otoh Raban]]
Hoxie was a [[Ankura/Legends|Ankura]] [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] who served as the [[Boss/Legends|Boss]] of the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Otoh Raban]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] in [[3000 BBY]].<ref name="EA" /> At that [[Time/Legends|time]], the Gungan tribes were at [[War of the Gungan tribes|war]] with each other, which eventually resulted in Hoxie being [[Kidnapping of Hoxie|kidnapped]] and placed in a [[Rogoe's stronghold|prison camp]] by the [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Rogoe/Legends|Rogoe]], who was controlling the war and wished for it to continue. For some time, Otoh Raban was left without a leader, and its inhabitants were forced to keep training soldiers to defend themselves from the other tribes.<ref name="SW:GB" />

However, [[Gallo/Legends|Gallo]], another Boss who was attempting to unite the Gungans against Rogoe, heard about Hoxie's kidnapping from a [[Unidentified Gungan villager (Otoh Raban)|villager]] living in Otoh Raban, and agreed to rescue him. After his ally [[Marsune (Gungan)|Marsune]] told him of rumors of the prison camp where Hoxie was being kept, Gallo led his small [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|army]] into the stronghold and attempted to rescue Hoxie. When they found him the Boss thanked Gallo for saving him, and told him Otoh Raban would join his crusade if he was rescued. Gallo's forces destroyed the prison camp and freed Hoxie, then proceeded to take him back to Otoh Raban. Upon returning him to the city, Hoxie pledged his allegiance to Gallo, and contributed four repeater [[Militiagung/Legends|militiagungs]], Gungans who fired repeating [[Booma/Legends|boomas]], to Gallo's army.<ref name="SW:GB" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Until hesa is returned safe and sound, it will be war for usen!|A villager living in Otoh Raban, speaking of Hoxie|<ref name="SW:GB" />}}
Hoxie served as the boss of Otoh Raban, and when he was kidnapped by Rogoe, his city refused to stop fighting the other Gungan tribes until he was rescued. Hoxie was very thankful when Gallo rescued him, and agreed to join Gallo's cause to defeat Boss Rogoe to show his gratitude. Hoxie was also a [[Color/Legends|green-skinned]] Ankura Gungan.<ref name="SW:GB" /> Hoxie was also a Gungan surname with an archaic etymology, with a literal meaning of "laughing [[King/Legends|king]]" that was a possible reference to some kind of court [[Jester (occupation)/Legends|jester]].<ref name="GCG">''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The boss of Otoh Raban was first mentioned in ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]'' by [[Steven L. Kent]],<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> released on [[November 7]], [[2001]].<ref name="PrimaDate">{{Amazon|url=Star-Wars-Galactic-Battlegrounds-Official/dp/0761537503|text=Star Wars Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide}}</ref> Hoxie then appeared and was identified in the [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'',<ref name="SW:GB" /> released on [[November 11]], 2001.<ref name="GBDate">{{GameSpot|url=games/star-wars-galactic-battlegrounds|text=Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds}}</ref> Hoxie appears in the second level of the Gungan campaign, "The Gungan who would be Boss," in which the player has to rescue him to complete the level. The image used for Hoxie in the game is the same as the other Gungan bosses that appear, and his voice actor was uncredited in the game.<ref name="SW:GB" />
